methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
ABL Bio is a publicly listed biotech company on KOSDAQ that has raised over $5 billion through IPO and licensing partnerships. The valuation universe comprises 11 comparable companies—3 public peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private firms including Tempus AI, Abridge, Ultima Genomics, Eikon Therapeutics, and Freenome—all operating in the Biotech & Health sector.

Standard triangulation methods are not applicable to this valuation. Peer-median analysis cannot proceed because the Biotech & Health peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables meeting minimum thresholds (need ≥3 datapoints; available narrow set = 0). Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples is also inapplicable because ABL Bio's revenue is not disclosed on file—a constraint typical of earlier-stage biotech entities, despite public listing status. The sector median P/S multiple of 7.4x across the three public tickers cannot be deployed without the target's revenue figure.

Without applicable triangulation, this analysis cannot support a quantitative valuation range. Resolution requires either: (1) disclosure or reliable estimation of ABL Bio's current or forward revenue; (2) identification of additional same-stage biotech comparables with sufficient data density; or (3) shift to alternative methods such as DCF or precedent transaction analysis, neither of which is supported by the data provided. Materiality of valuation uncertainty remains high pending revenue visibility.
